scope: M29 Stuart sign-in support and SMTP email OTP settings

productionDiagnosis.finding: Stuart is active and not OTP locked. No LoginOtp rows exist for Stuart, so the user likely did not complete a successful code request with the exact account email. SMS delivery to the same phone is working for HOT alerts. Email fallback now uses SMTP.

backend: AuthOtpService SMS OTP continues to use the existing Twilio client and active Messaging Service path.
AuthOtpService email OTP now sends through SMTP with local server defaults and external SMTP override settings.
SettingsController now whitelists smtpFromEmail, smtpHost, smtpPort, smtpUser, smtpPassword, and smtpSecure.
smtpPassword is masked as a sensitive setting.
smtpFromEmail, smtpPort, and smtpSecure are validated.
frontend: Settings -> Integrations now includes Email Sign-in / SMTP fields.
Login first screen now offers Send SMS code and Use email code instead after an email is entered.
Login footer copy now reflects phone or email code delivery.

validation.typeErrors: No errors found in changed files via VS Code diagnostics.
validation.focusedTests: server npm test -- --run tests/authOtpPolicy.test.ts tests/authDevModeLogin.test.ts: 2 files passed, 8 tests passed.
validation.build: npm run build passed: server tsc and client tsc/vite build.

steps: Opened login page in local Vite preview.
Confirmed Send SMS code is enabled.
Confirmed Use email code instead is enabled on the first screen.

remainingExternalDependency.serverSmtp: Production server must have a working SMTP relay, or admin must save external SMTP credentials in Settings.
remainingExternalDependency.smtpFromEmail: Client/admin must confirm the sender email/domain is authorized for SMTP delivery.
remainingExternalDependency.productionEmailSmoke: Must be run after SMTP relay or external SMTP credentials are ready.
